title: Add --proxy CLI flag and thread to commands::chat
description:
Add --proxy <url> global clap flag to nie CLI; resolve it from CLI arg or config.toml proxy field; pass it through commands::chat() to transport layer.

## What
Wire the proxy URL from CLI flag or config into the chat command so it reaches connect_with_retry().

## Algorithm
1. In cli/src/main.rs, in the Cli struct after the lightwalletd field, add:
   #[arg(long, global = true, help = 'SOCKS5 proxy URL (e.g. socks5h://127.0.0.1:9050 for Tor)')]
   proxy: Option<String>,
2. After the relay/lightwalletd resolution block (around line 106), add:
   let proxy = cli.proxy.or(file_config.proxy);
3. In the commands::chat() call, pass proxy as the new last argument.
4. In cli/src/commands.rs, add proxy: Option<String> as the last param to pub async fn chat().
5. In commands.rs line ~260, update connect_with_retry() call to pass proxy.clone().
6. Run: cargo build -p nie-cli exits 0; nie --help shows --proxy flag.

## Defensive programming rules
- Never log the proxy URL at info level or below (may contain credentials)
- clap help text must describe socks5h:// as recommended for .onion (Tor)
- proxy resolution order: CLI flag > config.toml key > None — same as relay and lightwalletd
- Do not validate the proxy URL in this bead — that is the validation bead's job

## Agent team
Researcher (read main.rs + commands.rs), Implementer, Validator

## Tests
Acceptance: cargo build -p nie-cli exits 0; nie --help output contains '--proxy'; cargo test --workspace exits 0.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-cli exits 0; --proxy appears in nie --help; cargo test --workspace exits 0
